title: InterpUpdates — Experiment Dashboard
subtitle: Interpretable fine-tuning updates: localizing & removing spurious-correlation shortcuts in LLMs

experiments.title: Exp 1 — C+/C− max-activating neuron interpretation
experiments.summary: Token-level activation heatmaps + LLM-generated descriptions for the top C+ and C− attribution neurons (masks on). Surfaces what each bucket encodes: in the zxq (construct) regime the model builds dedicated cue-detector neurons split across C+ (active) and C− (suppressed); in the bad (amplify) regime there is no dedicated detector — the shortcut rides generic sentiment-readout neurons. Tabs per model × correlate.

experiments.title: Exp 2 — Ablating the interpreted shortcut neurons
experiments.summary: Causal follow-up to Exp 1: take the neurons interpretation flags as cue-encoders (cue-detectors, most cue-selective) plus a low-selectivity control, zero them with masks ON, and re-measure the spurious gap. Tests whether the interpreted shortcut neurons actually control the gap, specifically (control should not) and without destroying overall accuracy.

experiments.title: Exp 3 — Weight-side (ROTATE) validation of C+/C−
experiments.summary: Data-free, weight-only analysis (ROTATE-inspired, Lin et al.). Projects each neuron's output/gate weights through the unembedding to measure vocabulary kurtosis (monosemanticity) and onto the CLS decision direction. Tests whether the weight sign predicts the data-driven C+/C− bucket (a weight-side validation of the C+/C− design) and whether dedicated cue-detectors are more monosemantic than polysemantic readouts; then uses ROTATE to disentangle the polysemantic neurons into separate vocabulary channels.
